ai-w5-t1.answer: Optimization = make code faster or leaner **after** it works. Measure first. Premature optimization wastes time.
ai-w5-t1.realWorld: Working O(n²) for 100 rows ships; optimize when data grows.

ai-w5-t2.answer: Time complexity: how runtime grows with input size. O(1) constant, O(n) linear, O(n²) nested loops. Ask AI, then verify.
ai-w5-t2.realWorld: 10k users × nested loop = noticeable lag — HashMap lookup fixes it.

ai-w5-t3.answer: Space complexity: extra memory used. Trading space for time (cache, HashMap) is common and OK if memory fits.
ai-w5-t3.realWorld: Store seen IDs in HashSet — O(n) space for O(1) lookup.

ai-w5-t4.answer: Paste slow method + input size. Ask for bottleneck and fix. Time both versions.
ai-w5-t4.realWorld: AI identifies repeated linear scan — you add index map and benchmark.

ai-w5-t5.answer: AI suggests test cases; you write assertions. Focus on boundaries: 0, 1, max, duplicates.
ai-w5-t5.realWorld: Collection tests: empty, single element, duplicates, null element if allowed.

ai-w5-t6.answer: Ask which collection fits: random access → ArrayList; frequent insert middle → LinkedList; fast lookup → HashMap.
ai-w5-t6.realWorld: Counting frequencies → HashMap<String,Integer>, not nested loops.

ai-w5-t7.answer: Readable beats clever for team code. Ask AI to simplify over-nested loops if performance allows.
ai-w5-t7.realWorld: Extract filter logic to named method — next dev thanks you.

ai-w5-t8.answer: AI review for performance smells: nested loops, boxing in tight loops, wrong collection choice.
ai-w5-t8.realWorld: Autoboxing in hot loop — AI flags; you use primitive int array.
